content.problemStatement:
Chef's phone has a total storage of SSS MB. Also, Chef has 222 apps already installed on his phone which occupy XXX MB and YYY MB respectively.

He wants to install another app on his phone whose memory requirement is ZZZ MB. For this, he might have to delete the apps already installed on his phone. Determine the minimum number of apps he has to delete from his phone so that he has enough memory to install the third app.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains four integers S,X,YS, X, YS,X,Y and ZZZ — the total memory of Chef's phone, the memory occupied by the two already installed apps and the memory required by the third app.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum number of apps Chef has to delete from his phone so that he can install the third app.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers S X Y Z — total storage of the phone, memory occupied by the first installed app, memory occupied by the second installed app (X ≤ Y), and memory required by the new app.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line — the minimum number of already installed apps Chef has to delete (0, 1 or 2) so that the new app can be installed.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: free = 10-(1+2)=7 ≥ 3 → 0 deletions.
Test case 2: free = 9-(4+5)=0 < 1, after deleting the larger app (5) free = 9-4=5 ≥ 1 → 1 deletion.
Test case 3: free = 15-(5+10)=0 < 15, after deleting one app free = 15-5=10 < 15 → need to delete both → 2 deletions.
Test case 4: free = 100-(20+30)=50 ≥ 75? No, after deleting the larger app (30) free = 100-20=80 ≥ 75 → 1 deletion.
Free memory = 20-(5+5)=10 which is already ≥ required 5, so no app needs to be deleted.
Free = 10-(4+5)=1 < 10. Deleting the larger app (5) leaves free = 10-4=6 < 10, so both apps must be removed → 2 deletions.

hidden.label: edge case – exact fit without deletion

solution.approach: For each test case compute the currently free memory: free = S - (X + Y). If free ≥ Z, no deletion is needed → answer 0. Otherwise try deleting exactly one app. Deleting the larger app Y leaves only X on the phone, giving free = S - X. If this is ≥ Z, answer 1. If even after deleting the larger app there is not enough space, both apps must be removed → answer 2. The algorithm runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.
